rulings.Case Title: SUSAN MAYO et al VS. CADELINIA & LEUNG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint For Indemnity And Declaratory Relief
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 1: CROSS COMPLAINANT D.P.P.M., INC. DBA ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint For Indemnity And Declaratory Relief: GRANTED. Granting the motion will not cause prejudice to any party. See C.C.P. § 473(a)(1); Mesler v. Bragg Management Co. 39 Cal.3d 290, 296 (1985). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT GHIRARDELLI ACQUISITION COMPANY's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Gsquared Restaurant Ventures, Llc: OFF CALENDAR for failure to comply with SF Local Rules, Rule 2.6B. Parties failed to provide courtesy copies to Department 501. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA T LOPEZ et al VS. U.S. BANK , N.A. AS TRUSTEE FOR THE SARM 2006-8 et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT U.S. BANK and WELLS FARGO BANK's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to address all issues raised on demurrer. Plaintiff failed to file opposing papers and failed to provide courtesy copies to Department 501 per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RICKESH PATEL VS. FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTS FRANK MYERS and PLACE OF FRANKS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to Third Cause of Action: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff does not plead facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for Violation of SFRO section 37.10B. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN HARRIS et al VS. ALAN D SCHNEIDER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT ALAN SCHNEIDER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED in part, OVERRULED in part. Defendant's Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to the Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress Cause of Action, Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress Cause of Action, and Negligence Cause of Action. Plaintiff filed a non-opposition and requested the Court to dismiss these causes of action. Defendant's Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the Unlawful Eviction Cause of Action. Delayed discovery rule applies. See Sylve v. Riley (1993) 15 Cal. App. 4th 23. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVE ALLEN VS. ANGELA ARMSTRONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 11: DEFENDANT ANGELA ARMSTRONG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff: DENIED. Plaintiff's responses were proper because failure to pay rent is not alleged in the Notice to Quit. Furthermore, Plaintiff did provide responses to Interrogatories 81.1 through 8.13. Defendant to pay sanctions in the amount of $250. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CLAY GO et al VS. HENRY S GO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltff; And To Continue Trial Date; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 12: DEFENDANT HENRY GO's Motion To Compel Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltff; And To Continue Trial Date: GRANTED. Defendant properly served Plaintiff with interrogatories and requests for production of documents but Plaintiff failed to respond. Defendant's Motion for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Hearing Required for Defendant's motion to continue trial. = (501/REQ)
